name: Hong Kong Style French Toast - 2 Ways
description: A recent trip to Hong Kong had us eating this most amazing of French Toasts. Both are great versions, just depends on your mood: PB vs Beef Satay :)

ingredients.ingredient: slices milk bread, or any good white bread
steak (flank or skirt would be great) cut into thin strips
Milk bread, or any good white bread

step_sections.name: Peanut Butter
steps: Heat about an inch of oil in a skillet to 350 degrees
Spread peanut butter on 2 slices of the bread, then stack them and put the 3rd slice on top
Cut off crusts, dip in beaten egg then carefully add to the oil
Cook about 2 minutes a side, then each of the 4 edges until golden brown
Remove to a rack for a few seconds, then plate with a pat of butter and a drizzle of the sweetened condensed milk

step_sections.name: Satay Beef Stuffed
steps: Combine all satay ingredients (except oil & butter) in a bowl and mix well - refrigerate an hour
When ready to cook, add a tablespoon of neutral oil to a hot pan, then add about 1/2 the beef
Cook, stirring quickly until done, this should take no more than a minute - set aside and repeat with remaining beef
When ready to make the French toast, heat about an inch of oil in a skillet to 350 degrees
Put about 3 tablespoons of the cooled beef on one slice of bread, top with the other slice
Cut off crusts, dip in beaten egg then carefully add to the oil
Cook about 2 minutes a side, until golden brown
Remove to a rack for a few seconds, then plate with a pat of butter
